title: 23 Paskal Shopping Center
address: Jl. Pasir Kaliki No.25-27, Kebon Jeruk, Andir, Bandung City, West Java 40241
plus_code: 3HMV+VR Kebon Jeruk, Bandung City, West Java
description: Contemporary, multi-level shopping mall with well-known retailers, plus a cinema & food court.

user_reviews.Name: Kulineryan
user_reviews.Description:
One of the most popular malls in Bandung right now. It’s almost always packed, especially on weekends.

The mall is large, bright, and clean, making it comfortable to stroll around and shop. There’s a wide range of tenants, from mid-range brands to higher-end ones. There's also cinema too.

The layout is easy to understand, so you won’t easily get lost. Signage is also clear and helpful.

The main downside is parking. It’s often difficult to find a spot due to how crowded it gets. There’s also a large area usually reserved for valet parking, which feels like a waste of space, especially during peak hours.

user_reviews.Name: Ferdinand
user_reviews.Description:
Strategically located next to train station and Hilton hotel, I think this is the newest mall in Bandung.
Mall it self clean, spacious and full of tenant  from local and international brand like H&M, Uniqlo, etc.

user_reviews.Name: Jeanny W
user_reviews.Description: One of the largest malls in Bandung. It's busy, clean, and well-stocked. Parking can get a bit crowded on weekends. Although the parking lot is large, you can also park outdoors. Surrounding the mall are numerous shophouses and a food market. So after you leave the mall, you can wander around the shophouses, cafes, restaurants, and food market. The vibe is great here.

user_reviews.Name: J. Lepianda
user_reviews.Description: I’m usually not a fan of shopping malls, but I actually like this one. The size is manageable and not too overwhelming, making it comfortable to walk around. The only downside is the lobby area smelled quite strongly of cigarette smoke.
user_reviews.text_original: I’m usually not a fan of shopping malls, but I actually like this one. The size is manageable and not too overwhelming, making it comfortable to walk around. The only downside is the lobby area smelled quite strongly of cigarette smoke.

user_reviews.Name: Iskandar Zulkarnain
user_reviews.Description:
The mall is strategically located and good, complete and also large.
Many choices of food, some are indoor and also outdoor.
The mall concept is cool with a beautiful interior. There are 3 floors with very good layout, it seems very productive.
Many famous brands such as Levi's, H&M, Rolex, Uniqlo, and many more. There are also many places to eat & drink with selected tenants.
One more thing, there is a very good ‘executive’ prayer room, the cleanliness of the toilet, ablution place and prayer place is maintained very well. Thank you to the management.
Enjoy the photos & videos.

The only thing that lacks is no dept store…, but overall IT’S LIKE #1 place in Bandung tbh

user_reviews.Name: Trisye Darmento
user_reviews.Description:
I recently visited 23 . In my opinion, it is truly the best shopping center in town. Spacious and bright aesthetic that feels welcoming.

Shopping: They have an incredible mix of brands and affordable retailers.

Dining: The food court and restaurant options are diverse, ranging from quick bites to fine dining, including wonderful local cuisine options.

Amenities: I love the clean facilities and the variety of entertainment, including a great cinema and a themepark for children.

It is definitely the perfect spot for both a quick shopping trip and a full day out with family or friends. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.text_translated:
I just found an Alfamart with really bad service, substandard, especially the female cashier, who forgets the date.
I'm often robbed of my money when buying coffee beans.
The product description clearly states 2 shots of coffee, but in the store, I'm given 1 shot.
When I asked why there's so little coffee,
the cashier replied, "That's the amount."
At Indomaret, you can request coffee: strong/regular, sweeter/less sugar.
Please provide more training to Alfamart Express cashiers.

user_reviews.Name: Hans Jonathan
user_reviews.Description: Went here on a rainy day, parking was troublesome, as it is located on a crowded street. They have 1 long table inside in case you want to eat the buns immediately. We inspect the showcase with lots of big buns. The price was 15-18K for big buns, and we just tried every one of them. We enjoyed their fish cakes too, it was big and plump. The owner is kind and friendly enough to let us eat there while waiting for the rain to pass.

user_reviews.Name: Candy
user_reviews.Description:
I ordered 2 boxes @10 baos, and for each box i ordered 3 chicken baos, but instead I received 3 chocolate baos and was still charged the price for chicken baos. This was very disappointing because I did not get what I ordered.
The service was also not very friendly, which made the experience even worse. I hope the staff can be more careful with orders and improve their customer service in the future.

user_reviews.Name: Hafizuddin Halid
user_reviews.Description:
Clean and comfy place with complete facilities. Worth the price. It’s about 15 minutes from Stasiun Bandung. The only downside is there’s no convenience store nearby, but you can still get quick snacks from the receptionist.

Braga Street is around a 20-minute walk, but the traffic can be hectic, especially during peak hours. I’d recommend taking a GoBike as it only takes about 10 minutes. The area is busy during the day and gets quiet at night. Overall, a solid choice for a short stay.

user_reviews.Name: Bebek
user_reviews.Description:
Overall, a comfortable stay with friendly and helpful staff. The pods themselves are comfortable, and the location is excellent—right opposite a mall, extremely close to an ATM, and within walking distance of several local restaurants and cafés.

One downside compared to other Bobopod locations is the lack of a barcode system for the shoe and sandal lockers. Instead, they use a key-operated locker. While this works fine most of the time, it becomes inconvenient when two people want to go out separately, as only one person can carry the key. It would be much more seamless if they implemented the same barcode locker system used at other Bobopod branches.

The rooftop area is quite nice, and features a kitchen like most other Bobopod branches, however it’s also somewhat inconvenient to have to go all the way upstairs just to clean plates or utensils. Other Bobopod locations I’ve stayed at had kitchens on the ground or first floor, which felt much more practical. The musholla is also located on the top floor, which adds to this inconvenience.

Another small but annoying issue is that every time you want to access the rooftop area (kitchen, musholla, etc.), you have to exit into the main landing and then scan your barcode again to re-enter the pods area. At other Bobopod locations, the entire common area is usually behind a single barcode door, allowing you to move freely around the hotel in the provided clean sandals without repeatedly scanning in and out.

The bathroom was generally fine, though it wasn’t always as clean as other Bobopod locations in the area.

Overall, this is a good Bobopod with a great location and friendly staff, but a few layout and system choices make it less convenient than other branches.

user_reviews.Name: Silvia Anggelina
user_reviews.Description:
(imo) The place is really strategic - near Bandung station, alun-alun, the kings mall (dept store) and you can find lot of eating destination around like Jl Cibadak, Gardujati, Braga and Sudirman street.

Pod concept is really unique, really utilized small spaces with compact layout and needed. You can book single - double to king size bed (mine right now is king) with affordable price.
Yes it's not large, very minimal layout - only space to put luggage, and bed to rest. but i think it's very enough for traveller who looking for a good night stay without much gimmick. (and doesn't mind having shared bathroom)

The room is digital concept (connected with their Bobocabin apps to lock, unlock, connect with staff, and checkout)
Overall the places, room and bathroom is clean. They also provide sandals and basic toileteries (toothbrush set & towels)

The staff is friendly!

A bit concern maybe since it's a pod concept room - not like your typical room building at the hotel- it's not soundproof.

user_reviews.Name: francois cew
user_reviews.Description:
Canary Bakery and Cafe

* Meet Uncle Lim, a very friendly and humble owner. He taught us everything about coffee and also shared his experience with us.
* He also invited us to do free ‘sipping coffee’ which is a very unique & exciting experience for us.
* You can buy lots of coffee beans here, more than 30 varieties available.
* The shop also sell traditional bread, cakes and snacks.
* Open 6 am in the morning and close at 5 pm.
* Location at Braga intersection, in front of de Braga Hotel.
* Thank you so much for the free gift ( Flores beans ). Hope we can meet and have a good conversation again next time. Good luck Uncle Lim 🙏

user_reviews.Name: Karen Simmonds
user_reviews.Description: Top spot for a coffee and cake! They grind the beans right here on the premises, easily the best cup we have had since travelling in Indo for two months. We totally recommend and will visit again when next in Bandung.

user_reviews.Name: Martias Mukus
user_reviews.Description:
Tolong dong tim manager .. dikasih surat peringatan teteh kacamata, kejadiannya  di tgl 29, sore hari  posisi lagi ujan main lah ke circle sambil order dan duduk di meja buat berteduh. . Ya kalo makanan dan minuman udah abis kemesannya di buang dong ke tong sampah. nah selang satu jam kemudian datanglah si teteh buat nyapu dan bersihin tempat meja, .. ngak tau ya kenapa harus ke meja saya. asbak yang saya pakai dua" nya buat merokok dipindahin oleh dia ke meja coustomer lain, terus.. saya kaget dong dengan nada nyolotnya "ayo kak silahkan di order di dalam" lah harus dua kali ordernya. dua kali dia nyolot ke saya.  malu cuy dilihatin sama coustomer lain malah banyak orang lagi..
malunya itu loh ngak bisa diterima.
user_reviews.text_original:
Tolong dong tim manager .. dikasih surat peringatan teteh kacamata, kejadiannya  di tgl 29, sore hari  posisi lagi ujan main lah ke circle sambil order dan duduk di meja buat berteduh. . user_reviews.text_translated:
Please help the manager team.. the glasses sister was given a warning letter, the incident happened on the 29th, in the afternoon it was raining, she went to the circle while ordering and sat at the table for shelter. . Yes, if the food and drinks were finished, the order was thrown into the trash. Well, an hour later the sister came to sweep and clean the table, .. I don't know why she had to come to my table. The ashtrays that I used for smoking were moved by her to another customer's table, then .. I was shocked by her loud tone "come on sis, please order inside" she had to order twice. twice she glared at me. It was embarrassing, man, being seen by other customers, and there were even more people. 
That embarrassment is unacceptable.

user_reviews.text_translated:
Hi sis, I bought this yesterday at Korner Cihampelas, the first time I drank it was at home after breaking the fast but the tea tasted stale but I denied it because I thought it was the wrong taste and made an untrue accusation then I put it in the fridge and tried it again after eating sahur and it turned out to be stale, the taste was even stronger. 
I mean is there no quality control? Double check? The tea is not changed every day? I even regretted not drinking it where I broke the fast, this is the first time I was disappointed that my favorite drink was tainted, oh yeah, get used to it when you have guests, don't chat too loudly, especially when discussing salaries

user_reviews.Name: Varenzora.
user_reviews.Description:
#Lokasinya seberang Hotel Savoy Hooman dan Museum Konferensi Asia Afrika. Tempat andalan ketika lagi main di Braga karena menu dan harganya sangat kantong pelajar. Terlebih banyak promo dan diskon yang mantap. Sayang banget, dulu area dine in nya cukup luas, tetapi setelah direnovasi jadi cuma bangku panjang yang berhadapan langsung dengan jalan raya.

#Menunya bervariatif dan harganya terjangkau. Saya sarankan beli menu paketan karena lebih murah dan puas. Paling suka siomay goreng dsn burritos nya.

#Pelayanannya sangat baik. Pegawainya juga ramah dan sangat membantu. Pertahankan terus. Terima kasih.
user_reviews.text_original:
#Lokasinya seberang Hotel Savoy Hooman dan Museum Konferensi Asia Afrika. Tempat andalan ketika lagi main di Braga karena menu dan harganya sangat kantong pelajar. Terlebih banyak promo dan diskon yang mantap. Sayang banget, dulu area dine in nya cukup luas, tetapi setelah direnovasi jadi cuma bangku panjang yang berhadapan langsung dengan jalan raya.

#Menunya bervariatif dan harganya terjangkau. Saya sarankan beli menu paketan karena lebih murah dan puas. Paling suka siomay goreng dsn burritos nya.

#Pelayanannya sangat baik. Pegawainya juga ramah dan sangat membantu. Pertahankan terus. Terima kasih.
user_reviews.text_translated:
#Located across from the Savoy Hooman Hotel and the Asian-African Conference Museum. It's a favorite spot when I'm in Braga because the menu and prices are very affordable for students. Plus, there are lots of great promotions and discounts. It's a shame that the dine-in area used to be quite spacious, but after renovations, it's just long benches facing the main road.

#The menu is varied and affordable. I recommend getting the set menu because it's cheaper and more satisfying. I especially like the fried siomay and burritos.

#The service is excellent. The staff are friendly and very helpful. Keep it up. Thank you.

user_reviews.text_translated:
One of the minimarkets located on Asia Afrika Street in the Braga area of ​​Bandung, with its distinctive Dutch architectural ornamentation.

It's strategically located near an intersection. On the right is the Asia Afrika Museum, across from the Savoy Homann Hotel, and directly next door is Starbucks.

It offers a wide selection of food and soft drinks, plus classic outdoor seating for enjoying the atmosphere of Braga Old Town.

user_reviews.Description:
Tetiba laper di jam 10 pagi..wkwkwk, nemu gerai circle K donk,,sebelahan ama KFC setiabudi, iseng masuk sini mau liat ada apa didalam..

untuk makanan nya, ada di riview dibawah ini yaa
1. Kebab
Jujur aja g punya ekspektasi tinggi untuk kebab ala2 supermarket begini, secara dulu pernah nyobain ditempat lain, yah begitulahh. Dan supriseee..ENAK PAKE BINGIT!
Berasa worth it banget lah ngeluarin duit 22k dapet kebab sama ice lemon tea ini. Isiannya itu loh, melimpah, trs potongan dagingnya gede2, bukan kayak daging kebab yg slice tipis2 gt, ini gemuk2 kayak potongan beef patty nya burger, rempahnya juga lezatos...terbaik ini mah! Repeat order pastiiii

Pelayanan nya ramah, pembayaran juga bisa by aplikasi

user_reviews.text_translated:
Suddenly hungry at 10 am... lol, I found a Circle K outlet, right next to KFC Setiabudi, and I went in to see what was inside.
The menu is extensive and varied, and although the prices are quite high, they also offer discounts.
You can see the menu and prices in the picture.
I happened to order the 6-piece package, which included a kebab with free lemon tea, and I also wanted to try the Tuna Mayo Onigiri. The total cost was only 30,800 Rupiah.
As for the food, see the review below.
1. Kebab
Honestly, I didn't have high expectations for this supermarket-style kebab, as I've tried it elsewhere before, so that's it. And surprise... IT'S SO DELICIOUS!
It felt totally worth it to spend 22,000 Rupiah on this kebab and iced lemon tea. The filling is abundant, and the meat pieces are big, not like thinly sliced ​​kebab meat, these are plump like burger beef patties, and the spices are delicious too...this is the best! I'll definitely order again!
2. Iced Lemon Tea
For someone with a sensitive digestive system like me, this tea is actually delicious, but afterward, my stomach starts to feel queasy, so I stop.
The lemon aroma is great, the taste is refreshing, and it's not too sweet either. Try it, you'll definitely be hooked. It's a shame I couldn't try more. Sob.
3. Tuna Mayo Onigiri
No...I really liked it...lol, the nori felt like I was chewing paper, it was dry. The contents taste good, but just looking at them makes me lose my appetite...lol
Circle K Setiabudi also has a BCA ATM, so you don't have to worry about running out of cash when buying snacks.
The service is friendly, and you can also pay via the app.
The menu is varied and looks delicious. I'm curious. The packaged snacks also have a lot of variations. Plus, they have Walls and Glico Wings ice cream.
Conclusion: If you're looking for breakfast or a snack, this is a good option.

Making a sausage burger on the grill for 35 minutes, and then clicking "ready" on the order. This restaurant is bad, and just because it's in the driver's hands, they can do whatever they want. This has happened several times and is often the case.

Usually, when there's an order, they make it immediately, not waiting for the driver to make it. Drivers also sometimes get double orders, and when they get to the second restaurant, they haven't made it yet, which is a waste of time.

Hi admin, good afternoon. I had a snack at CK Corner next to the post office in Asia Afrika Bandung. I apologize, but the drink seemed to be under-served. Some of the syrup was too much, causing us to choke and cough from the sweetness. It made my throat itchy and felt like there was sediment. I've bought it at Lengkong several times, but it's delicious. The sweet and sour taste is just right. At Circle K Asia Afrika next to the post office, it's terrible.

The fishcake soup was very cold, and the pao was also cold and hard. The cleanliness of the restaurant is also not up to scratch. Please be more thorough.

user_reviews.text_translated:
I'm so sorry, I just bought a food promo on the display (bought ekado + karage) for a total of 18 thousand. At first I didn't suspect anything, but when I ate it, it turned out the ekado was stale (there was slime, an unpleasant smell, and the taste wasn't fresh either) T_T I just realized maybe I finished yesterday's stock..
Please make sure again not to sell food that is not suitable, thank you.

When I entered, I was immediately greeted by the person in charge who was friendly and attracted to the iced coffee which was on promotion. When I asked how long it would last, the person in charge answered until it was finished and spontaneously laughed there. The coffee was delicious and refreshing, especially when it was hot, huuuu.

Buka 24 jam, barang cukup lengkap hanya saja harga sedikit lebih mahal jika dibandingkan dengan minimarket waralaba lain.
user_reviews.text_original: Letaknya strategis, di belokan ujung Jalan Dipati Ukur menuju Ir. H. Djuanda. Dulu sering digunakan untuk potong jalan, tapi sekarang jalan yang di Dago sudah ditutup dan dipakai untuk shuttle travel. Buka 24 jam, barang cukup lengkap hanya saja harga sedikit lebih mahal jika dibandingkan dengan minimarket waralaba lain.
user_reviews.text_translated: It's strategically located at the turnoff at Jalan Dipati Ukur towards Ir. H. Djuanda. It used to be a shortcut, but now the Dago road has been closed and is used for shuttle services. Open 24 hours, the store has a fairly complete selection of goods, although prices are slightly higher than other franchise minimarkets.

user_reviews.Name: Marchellin Antonia
user_reviews.Description:
Located on Jalan Sulanjana, this is one of the larger Circle K stores in Bandung. It offers a spacious parking area, which is super convenient, and a smoking area complete with tables and chairs—perfect for sitting down and enjoying your food or coffee.

I tried the kebab and iced coffee, and both were surprisingly good. A nice spot for a quick, casual hangout.

user_reviews.Name: Makna Nur Makrifah
user_reviews.Description:
Tempat makanan sunda di dekat alun-alun. Ke sini karena ada konten di tiktok dengan pembawaan yang bagus. namun sesampainya di tempat, biasa aja, bahkan zonk. Banyak lalat dan aroma tidak sedap dari bagian belakang sangat menggangu.
di bagian tempat cuci tangan, ada lap yang sudah kotor dan juga bau. sebaiknya kalau mau memang disediakan lap tangan, harus siap juga ganti atau sparenya. lalu ditengah-tengah lagi makan dikerumuni lalat, salah satu pegawainya menyemprotkan pengharum ruangan di sekeliling. 😔 harusnya kondisi tsb sudah diantisipasi sebelum pengunjung datang.
lalu dibagian lalapan, terong hijaunya kurang segar, timun dikerumuni lalat dan tidak ditutup, lalu sayur seladanya tampak masih banyak tanahnya. ga mungkin dong saya ambil dan cuci sendiri :')
lalu melihat harga dengan makanan yang biasa, namun service dan suasananya tidak sebanding, bikin tambah jengkel 🥲
mohon dievaluasi dan diperbaiki ya, akang teteh.
Terima kasih.

user_reviews.text_translated:
Sundanese food place near the town square. I came here because of TikTok content with a good presentation. However, upon arrival, it was mediocre, even a dud. There were lots of flies and an unpleasant odor from the back was very disturbing.
At the handwashing area, there was a dirty and smelly cloth. If you want hand towels provided, you should be prepared to replace them or have spares. Then, in the middle of eating, flies swarmed around, and one of the employees sprayed air freshener around the area. 😔 This situation should have been anticipated before customers arrived.
Then, in the fresh vegetables section, the green eggplant wasn't fresh, the cucumber was swarmed with flies and uncovered, and the lettuce still looked like it had a lot of dirt on it. There was no way I would have picked it up and washed it myself :')
Then, seeing the prices for the mediocre food, but the service and atmosphere weren't commensurate, it made me even more annoyed 🥲
Please evaluate and improve, bro.
Thank you.

user_reviews.Description: Tempat makan prasmanan enak yang kayanya belum terlalu banyak yang tau di Bandung. Kesini kemarin pelanggan cuman rombongan kita aja. Makanan pilih sendiri nanti diangetin, kalo semacam pepes sama oseng oseng gitu ngga sih. Harganya murah, ber 6 cuman habis 350rban. Sambelnya bisa bikin sendiri, atau ambil yg udah dibikinin aja. Sayurannya pareot gaes seperti enggan hidup. Minusnya tempat parkirnya susah. Dia ada 2 muka gitu lho, entah nyambung apa ngga.

user_reviews.text_translated:
Got Pranked with a Promo! As the name suggests, Kabita Ku offers a package price of Rp 15,000. The banner says Rp 15,000. When I wanted to order, it turned out to be sold out... I'd already gone in and asked... I just went in and ordered something else. As a result, one portion cost me around Rp 30,000.
The handwashing area and water tap were dirty.
The waiter told me to take the vegetables and eat them at the back table.
It turned out there were no vegetables available, only the chili sauce... Very disappointed, the food tasted mediocre.
The table was dirty, covered in tissues and food scraps. The waiter didn't immediately clean them, so we had to clean the table.
The plates on the table were also questionably clean, so I chose plates from another table. The plates on the table were already facing upwards, looking like they were dirty and dusty.

title: ESSEL BANDUNG - PASIR KALIKI
address: Jl. Dr. Rajiman No.1, Pasir Kaliki, Kec. Cicendo, Kota Bandung, Jawa Barat 40171

user_reviews.Name: Bella Lolytha
user_reviews.Description:
Absolutely loved this place! The rose toppoki is seriously so good, rich, creamy, and super satisfying. The kimbap on its own is pretty standard, but once you dip it into the sauce, it tastes amazing.
The only downside is the portion size. It’s quite big and more suitable for sharing. Not really ideal if you just want a solo “me time” snack, because it might be too much to finish alone.
user_reviews.text_original:

user_reviews.Name: Marchella Nurazzybach
user_reviews.Description:
Essel day✨
finally tried Essel Pasir Kaliki and of course… i ordered way too much. original tteokbokki, cream cheese gimbap, gimmari, eomuk, plus peach tea & soda to balance it all.
everything felt warm, simple, and comforting. waitres helpful & friendly.
the space was a little crowded, a little noisy… but still had that soft cozy charm in between moments💖

user_reviews.Name: Maharani Putri
user_reviews.Description:
Finally in Bandung! I’ve been a fan of their kimbap for ages, and I’m so thrilled they’ve opened here. I genuinely think they’re among the best for delicious and affordable Korean food.

You have to try their kimbap and tteokbokki—they’re absolutely top-notch! The place itself is adorable, clean, and gives off those simple, cozy Korean aesthetic vibes. Definitely planning to come back and explore more of their menu. Highly recommend!🙌🏻

user_reviews.Name: Januar Fahmi
user_reviews.Description:
Lokasi gerai cukup terpencil, jadi agak susah mencarinya.
Lokasinya pas depan Miniapolis, jika dari arah lift, posisi gerai kurang terlihat.

Untuk makanannya salah satu yang direkomendasikan oleh kawan yaitu Varial Double Mozza. Dimana kerju mozanya melar banget (dikonsumsi tidak jauh dari waktu penyajian).

Serta varian manis yang Hazzlenut, penuh taburan coklat serta Hazzlenut jam nya sangat cocok bagi penyuka makanan manis.

Waktu tunggu penyajian kurang lebih 10 menit.
user_reviews.text_original:
Lokasi gerai cukup terpencil, jadi agak susah mencarinya.
Lokasinya pas depan Miniapolis, jika dari arah lift, posisi gerai kurang terlihat.

Untuk makanannya salah satu yang direkomendasikan oleh kawan yaitu Varial Double Mozza. Dimana kerju mozanya melar banget (dikonsumsi tidak jauh dari waktu penyajian).

Serta varian manis yang Hazzlenut, penuh taburan coklat serta Hazzlenut jam nya sangat cocok bagi penyuka makanan manis.

user_reviews.text_translated:
The outlet's location is quite secluded, so it's a bit difficult to find.

It's right in front of Miniapolis, and it's hard to see from the elevator.

One of the food recommendations from a friend is the Varial Double Mozza. The mozzarella cheese is incredibly stretchy (consumed shortly after serving time).

There's also the sweet Hazzlenut variant, topped with chocolate and Hazzlenut jam, perfect for those with a sweet tooth.

The wait time is approximately 10 minutes.

user_reviews.Name: Fiona
user_reviews.Description: Lokasi di dalam paskal 23. Lokasinya dekat tempat main anak. Cukup mudah menemukannya. Harga makanan dan rasa sebanding mengingat ini ada dalam mall. Cukup terhibur dengan melihat bagaimana proses memasaknya.

user_reviews.Name: Handi Suyadi
user_reviews.Description:
Karakter Rasa Bukan untuk Semua

(Beli pada 2 Maret 2024)

Gamchi termasuk penjual Korean Potato Bread yang populer. Mengklaim diri sebagai penjual pertama mungkin berperan dalam menarik perhatian konsumen. Sebab sejauh ini belum ada kompetitor yang sepadan dengan Gamchi.

Terdapat berbagai varian rasa yang ditawarkan. Hanya saja, masih diperlukan diferensiasi produk lebih jelas agar kue mudah dibedakan.

Gamchi Ubee (5/10)
Sayang sekali rasa ube sama sekali tidak terasa. Rasanya nyaris tak ada bedanya dengan Gamchi Double Mozza. Hanya warna ungu sebagai pembeda. Dapat diasumsikan kue tersa manis karena menggunakan ube, tapi rasa yang dapat dirasakan justru asin dan gurih. Sekalipun dikasih ube bubuk tetap tidak ada gunanya.
Setiap menu disajikan beserta gula halus dan ube bubuk.

Gamchi Double Mozza (8.5/10)
Double Mozza? Ya, banyak keju mozzarella pada permukaan kue. Karakter rasa kue yang asin dan gurih jauh lebih pas untuk menu ini.
Dibandingkan dengan Gamchi Ubee, rasa kue jauh lebih baik. Keberadaan keju mozzarella membuat kue menjadi gurih. Walaupun jumlahnya banyak, tidak bikin eneg. Tambah nikmat disantap dalam keadaan hangat.
Setiap menu disajikan beserta seledri kering, saus Korean Barbeque dan cabe bubuk.

Karakter rasa kue termasuk tipe yang belum tentu bisa cocok untuk semua orang. Tidak semua orang bisa menerima tekstur yang legit dan rasa yang tidak umum.

Setiap pembelian mendapat satu buah sarung tangan plastik.

user_reviews.text_translated:
Flavor Not for Everyone

(Purchased on March 2, 2024)

Gamchi is a popular seller of Korean Potato Bread. Claiming to be the first seller may have played a role in attracting consumers, as so far there haven't been any competitors comparable to Gamchi.

There are various flavors on offer. However, clearer product differentiation is needed to make the cakes easily distinguishable.

Gamchi Ubee (5/10)
Unfortunately, the ube flavor is completely absent. It tastes almost the same as Gamchi Double Mozza. The purple color is the only difference. One might assume the cake tastes sweet because of the ube, but the flavor is actually salty and savory. Even adding ube powder would be pointless.
Each dish is served with powdered sugar and ube powder.

Gamchi Double Mozza (8.5/10)
Double Mozza? Yes, there's plenty of mozzarella cheese on the surface of the cake. The salty and savory flavors of the cake are much more fitting for this dish.
Compared to Gamchi Ubee, the cake tastes much better. The mozzarella cheese adds a savory flavor. Despite the large amount, it's not overwhelming. It's even more delicious when eaten warm.
Each dish is served with dried celery, Korean barbecue sauce, and chili powder.

The cake's flavor isn't for everyone. Not everyone can handle the chewy texture and unusual flavor.

With each purchase, you'll receive a pair of plastic gloves.

user_reviews.Name: Fayna Faradiena
user_reviews.Description:
Came here for dinner and a little reunion with my best friends. We went on a Saturday night and since we already made a reservation, we got a table.

Even though it was the weekend, the place still felt comfortable (not overcrowded even when it was busy) thanks to the well-thought-out layout & setting!

The atmosphere is super homey and lush, making it such a warm spot to spend quality time with loved ones.

Service? TOP—every staff member was polite and SUPER FRIENDLY!! The cleanliness, comfort, and overall area are just excellent. Hutanika is also very kids-friendly and wheelchair-friendly.

As for the food—no doubt, everything was delicious, from local Indonesian dishes to Western options. With the quality and facilities you get, it’s definitely WORTH THE MONEY (though this time I was treated, haha). If you ask whether I’d come back to Hutanika? Definitely YES!!

One small note about the food: my husband and I tried the “Blackberry Cream Cheese” pizza. We were expecting the cream cheese to be more like the soft, cake-style version instead of savory pizza cheese, and it turned out to be a really unique taste! Both of us actually liked it, but we’d probably go for the more general pizza flavors next time.

*) You can celebrate your / your beloved birthday in here with customised platting of sliced cake & happy birthday sing from the friendly Hutanika team!💌🫶🏻

user_reviews.Name: Hans Jonathan
user_reviews.Description:
They presents a remarkable "one-stop visit" concept, transforming a vast expanse of land in the city center into a tropical forest escape. This unique use of Bandung's prime land space is a defining feature, a natural serene contrast to the urban landscape of the surrounding.

Despite the size, the place is packed with visitors on a weekend night. We are put on a waiting list for 10 minutes before being directed to our seats. Because of this and their location in the middle of crowded Asia Africa street, we choose to use ride hailing instead of driving here.
The establishment's extensive menu, featuring hundreds of items, is a testament to its comprehensive offerings. They have at least three separate kitchens and from there managed to offer a diverse culinary selection, ranging from classic Indonesian dishes to modern fusions.

From my recent visit, I ordered:
Indonesian rice sets such as Nasi Khas Hutanika and Nasi Bakar Telor Asin, and creative pasta dishes like Spaghetti Alfredo Gochujang and Creamy Pasta Baked Pot. Both was delicious, and well priced. The beverage menu is equally varied, with refreshing choices such as Strawberry Juice, and mocktails: Lychee Mojito, Virgin Mary, and Out of This World (non-alcoholic Mai Tai). The beautiful presentation of these drinks and dishes elevate the dining experience.

Beyond food, Hutanika is a hub of activities. It houses a boxing arena, billiard tables, gelato store and a playground for children. The well-designed space have some beautiful photo spots, including a charming outdoor area with a fountain and a pond that creates a calming ambiance. The atmosphere is further elevated by live music on weekends, starting from 8 PM onwards.

user_reviews.Name: Giofani Catalina
user_reviews.Description: I came here because I was attracted with the color and menu, and my eyes didn't deceive me. Jijou Coffee and Social is so far my favorite cafe in Bandung in terms of their concept. I tried their Ramadhan special beverage called Honey Chrysantemum and a mocktail called Tokyo Revenger. I really love Honey Chrysantemum. It was sweet and refreshing. Other than the beverages, I also play with their old arcade game machine. It costs 5k for 3 coins (3 lives). While I was sitting there, one of their staff came to me and ask me about how do I like the beverages. I think that's a really sweet and thoughtful move. Would totally come visit again and try other menu.

user_reviews.Description: Cozy backyard garden atmosphere serving very good taste Indonesian food like nasi nusantara, soto betawi and bebek panggang colo-colo (my recent visit). It also has various traditional kue kue and bread (bakery)

user_reviews.Name: Kevin World
user_reviews.Description:
A Taste of Home and Authentic Flavors!

We truly enjoyed our meal here. The "Nasi Campur Nyonya" was an absolute delight—it felt just like enjoying heartwarming, homemade dishes. We also tried the "Ikan Bumbu Nyonya," which offered a lovely, authentic flavor profile, though I wished the taste was a touch bolder to really sing. The "Nasi Iga Sapi Bakar" was satisfying, but a slight note: the accompanying soup was a bit on the salty side for my preference. Overall, the experience delivered genuine, authentic tastes. With a few minor tweaks to the seasoning, the food here could be truly outstanding!

Don't forget to try the traditional street snacks available right out front!

However it is worth with the taste and the experience.

user_reviews.Description:
Wow...I didn't realize that the restaurant has such a large area and can accommodate many costumer to dine in.

Delicious food and no disappointment for coming away from Jakarta and they services great too.

When I came in rainy condition, so the atmosphere was even more serene and romantic.

The parking lot doesn't fit many cars, but they provide valet service.

I recomended this retaurant if you visit to Bandung 👍

user_reviews.Description: I love going here just for the bakery since it's a walking distance from my in-law's home. They have many different kinds of breads. Prices were reasonable. You can also order the bread or cakes to be eaten with main course at the dining table and they will just put it on your tab accordingly. My only complaint is there's only one cashier to handle both the bakery and the restaurant, so sometimes long queue is unavoidable.

user_reviews.Name: Mr. Oktavianus
user_reviews.Description: I went to this famous restaurant in Bandung with a cozy Sundanese ambiance. They had some fresh bakery and many local traditional snacks at the entrance. The room was spacious, and surprisingly they even had live music during my visit. I tried their nasi tutug oncom gepuk, and it was delicious. The seasoning was on point, and the gepuk was tender. The only thing was, the sambal could've been spicier. As for the es tape kelapa muda, I wasn't a big fan. It was way too sweet, even though I asked for less sugar and didn't add any condensed milk.

user_reviews.Description: First time in Indonesia and my first 3 nights were in Jakarta and I could not find beer anywhere. Left and caught train to Bandung and checked into hotel close to station. Within a few minutes walk on the same road I found this beer shop 😄 It save me from 4 days boredom with no beer 🤣 Now I am back on my 2nd night 🍻 You can pay by Visa but minimum spend is 100k so I pay for 3 bottles of beer and ask them to keep in fridge as I drink outside in seated area one at a time. Friendly staff and I guess the prices are reasonable as I have no comparison (yet)

user_reviews.Name: Adrian Agoes
user_reviews.Description:
Modern, air-conditioned and inviting atmosphere of the mall was pleasantly surprising. The variety of stores is catered to a moderate class demographic, offering good quality clothing at affordable prices. The promotional offers available were appreciated, making the shopping experience even more enjoyable.

The layout of the mall made for comfortable walking and easy navigation, which is always a plus when shopping with family. The strategic location of Kings Shopping Center also made it convenient to access, adding to the overall positive experience.

Additionally, the comfortable parking facilities made it hassle-free to get in and out of the mall. Recommended for those looking for a pleasant shopping experience with good quality products at reasonable prices. There's even a covered bridge crossing the street from the parking lot to the mall main building.

user_reviews.Name: David Mundung
user_reviews.Description: The Kings Shopping Centre has almost everything you expect from a modern shopping center. There are a lot of clothing stores that cater from adults to children. There are also restaurants from various well known vendors and a food court to satisfy your culinary taste. There's also Azko and Mr. DIY in case you need tools and appliances for your house. What it does not have though, is a supermarket. This may be because of the Grand Yogya located next to the building. The biggest drawback of this shopping center though, is the parking. There's no parking available on the same building with this shopping center. You have to park your car on the parking building located across the shopping center. In order to reach the parking building, you must make sure to come from Jalan Dewi Sartika then go to Jalan Sampang. You cannot come from Jalan Otto Iskandar Dinata since there's a divider on the street in front of the Shopping Center separated the two sides of the street.

user_reviews.Name: Chrysan Lim
user_reviews.Description:
Non-halal
This is one of late grandma & dad fave food that become very rare in Jakarta,, we call it Kompiang. While in Bandung, I want to try it based on reviews in someone's igstory. Don't believe google map 😅. It actually direct you to the wrong alley. So i improvise by asking local people around. It's located in a narrow alley. It can be reach on foot, bike or motorcycle. So if u drive a car, park it somewhere else near the alley. I tried their Kompia 15K (chinese seaweed & pork filling) hard on the outside, so make sure you have strong teeth, Kompia plain 5K, Pork Ote-Ote 20K (very recommended), and sweet Kompia with 2 type of filling: 1. Nuts & raisin & 2. Tausa 45K. Will come back for Pork Kompia & Ote-Ote.

*Disclaimer: the review is based on my personal taste & experience at that time. So it might be differ from others opinion.

user_reviews.Name: Adinda Eine Azalia
user_reviews.Description:
Just tried their spicy tori baitan udon and it was really good (I asked to make it less spicy). The broth was very creamy, and the portion was more than enough for one person… especially if you’re really hungry. The price is reasonable for the taste and portion. Definitely worth the price…

The place itself was just okay.

However, I had a bad experience with the parking attendant. I was charged 5K for motorcycle parking, which I think is too much. There was no ticket, and it wasn’t a legal one.

Please don’t let issues like this make customers hesitate to come back. I believe many customers might feel the same. Hopefully, the parking situation can be handled better. Thank you.

user_reviews.Description: Ah, one of the legendary lomie stall in Bandung. Has been visiting this place during every visit to this city. The gravy is thick and flavorful. While the noodle is stretchy and smooth. A tad pricier, but you'll taste a classic taste.

user_reviews.Description: Not halal! One of the best noodle stall in bandung! Lomie Very tasty and fragrant. Yamien manis (sweet noodle) is good. Decent size. Pig ear is good too. Although in the higher price

user_reviews.Name: Andri Iesmail
user_reviews.Description:
I like the atmosphere of this place, it's brings back memories.
I like the food even more. One of the best 'nasi tim' in Bandung, it came with a rich taste clear soup and some delicious pickles.
Their lomie is delicious too, quite similar to other famous lomie in Bandung.
Their fried meatball has a crispy outer texture but soft and chewy and tasty in the inside.
One of the menu is 'Lumpia Shanghai' which is unlike any other lumpia i've ever tasted. It's delicious too btw

user_reviews.Description: Unfortunately they removed their ultra famous and special lomie benteng from their menu. user_reviews.Name: ALG87
user_reviews.Description:
A well known supermarket located at the basement of Festival Citylink, one of the malls at South Bandung. The location makes the place is strategic and easily accessible by vehicles. This place can be accessed by travelator, makes the place cozy for someone that buy many groceries. Many fresh products sold here.

A little bit disappointed for me that I can't bring my bag into this place while the others can. The security maybe feel suspicious to me, maybe, even I don't do something bad to them. The queues on the cashiers are long enough even in the evening and there are many cashiers that empty, so I should stand up for several minutes to wait for my turn.

user_reviews.Name: Syned
user_reviews.Description:
I absolutely love the yamien which is served here... the elasticity, taste and doneness of the noodle is just perfect!! The chilli sauce very tasty

If you want a yamien (noodle style) with marus (blood), meatball,  usus, and babat you have to visit this noodle house.

My wife said this place is her and her family favourite noodle shop in bandung and i think this noodle shop is one of legendary in bandung city.. my wife ate here since she was in middle school and after 10 years she absent to eat here, finaloy yesterday we eat the legendary noodle and she said the taste remain is not change and super delicious

Because of covid we decide to eat in the car.. well quite difficult but for safety i prefer to eat in the car

If you want a yamien (noodle style) with marus (blood), meatball,  usus, and babat you have to visit this noodle house.

My wife said this place is her and her family favourite noodle shop in bandung and i think this noodle shop is one of legendary in bandung city.. my wife ate here since she was in middle school and after 10 years she absent to eat here, finaloy yesterday we eat the legendary noodle and she said the taste remain is not change and super delicious

Because of covid we decide to eat in the car.. well quite difficult but for safety i prefer to eat in the car

Price ☆☆☆☆
Place ☆☆☆
Taste ☆☆☆☆☆
Safety for toddler or baby ☆☆

user_reviews.text_translated:
Try the coffee here! Order the Hot Cappuccino and Iced Irish Velvet. I love both, from the espresso latte to the balanced foam, and the delicious taste of the beans.

The Irish chocolate velvet is also fresh, and the Irish rhubarb is delicious.

The service is good, and the ambiance is clean and cozy. ✨✨✨

user_reviews.Name: Tiffany Murjadi
user_reviews.Description: Kopi susu signature nya enak bangett (lupa di foto 😭). Tipe kopi susu yang creamy, tapi tetep lekoh kerasa kopinya. Di atasnya ada whip cream manis (mirip kopi susu awan Kopi Toko Djawa), dan ada hint cinnamon nya. Nyobain earl grey tea, di atasnya dikasih whip cream juga, tapi whip cream nya asem seger gt unik. Ditambah ada parutan kulit lemon juga di atasnya, bikin aroma nya enak banget. Plus cobain matcha latte cuman 28rb dong, dimana matcha lain 45-50rb an. Definitely bakal balik lagi! 😍
user_reviews.text_original: Kopi susu signature nya enak bangett (lupa di foto 😭). Tipe kopi susu yang creamy, tapi tetep lekoh kerasa kopinya. Di atasnya ada whip cream manis (mirip kopi susu awan Kopi Toko Djawa), dan ada hint cinnamon nya. Nyobain earl grey tea, di atasnya dikasih whip cream juga, tapi whip cream nya asem seger gt unik. Ditambah ada parutan kulit lemon juga di atasnya, bikin aroma nya enak banget. Plus cobain matcha latte cuman 28rb dong, dimana matcha lain 45-50rb an. Definitely bakal balik lagi! 😍
user_reviews.text_translated: The signature milk coffee is really delicious (I forgot to take a picture 😭). A creamy type of milk coffee, but still has a strong coffee flavor. On top is sweet whipped cream (similar to Kopi Toko Djawa's cloud milk coffee), and there is a hint of cinnamon. Try the earl grey tea, topped with whipped cream too, but the whipped cream is sour and refreshing, unique. Plus there is grated lemon peel on top, making the aroma really delicious. Plus try the matcha latte for only 28 thousand rupiah, where other matchas are around 45-50 thousand rupiah. Definitely will come back again! 😍

user_reviews.Name: Gebyar Ahadiakbar Gustianadireja
user_reviews.Description: I popped into Oh Some! at Festival Citilink to get something fun for my child, and it’s quickly become our go-to spot for buildable toys. The shelves are filled with colourful, creative sets that spark imagination, and the staff are always friendly when I need suggestions. I love how safe, tidy, and well-organised the place feels, making it easy for my child to explore and choose. It’s honestly one of the most delightful toy shops for little builders in Bandung.

user_reviews.Description:
Amazing atmosphere with various stores left and right, from streetwear to a nail clinic; this cafe is versatile and completely like a mini mall in Bandung. The nasi goreng exceeded my expectations, the flavour is rich and savoury. Try the cireng, too, it's insanely crispy and delicious.

100% would recommend my friends and family to visit. Vibrant and lively cafe, never gets boring.

title: Papaya Fresh Gallery Bandung
address: Jl. Sukaasih No.1, Gegerkalong, Sukajadi, Bandung City, West Java 40141

user_reviews.Name: C A.H
user_reviews.Description: The only thing I regret is not having visited sooner! Love the variety of Japanese treats that they have. Their huge selection of mochis are 100/10, tastes very authentic. Their sushi is pretty decent for a store-made one; very fresh and good selection, very generous with the salmon portion. Their fruits are fresh. The place is clean. I dragged my mom here, who has lived in Bandung for many years and have yet to visit, but it's now a place she'll be returning to often. I will too everytime I'm in Bandung.
user_reviews.text_original: The only thing I regret is not having visited sooner! Love the variety of Japanese treats that they have. Their huge selection of mochis are 100/10, tastes very authentic. I dragged my mom here, who has lived in Bandung for many years and have yet to visit, but it's now a place she'll be returning to often. I will too everytime I'm in Bandung.

user_reviews.Name: Mita
user_reviews.Description:
Papaya Fresh Gallery in Bandung has become my go-to place for buying high-quality meat and fresh vegetables. The selection here is excellent, especially for those looking for premium cuts of meat and a wide variety of fresh produce. Everything is well-organized and easy to find, which makes shopping a breeze.

What I love most is the cleanliness of the store and the consistent quality of their products. The vegetables are always crisp and fresh, and the meat is packaged nicely, making it feel even more premium. They also carry a good selection of Japanese groceries, which is a bonus if you’re into cooking Japanese dishes.

The staff are friendly and helpful, always ready to assist if you need recommendations or help finding something. Although the prices are slightly higher than in other stores, I think it’s worth it for the quality and convenience.

If you’re looking for fresh ingredients and a pleasant shopping experience in Bandung, Papaya Fresh Gallery is definitely worth a visit!

user_reviews.Name: Edwin Indera
user_reviews.Description:
The selection of Japanese products, in particular, is impressive, making it a go-to spot for authentic ingredients. The store is clean and well-organized, creating a pleasant shopping experience. Whether you're looking for specialty items or just doing your weekly grocery run, Papaya Fresh Market has you covered.

This place always brings back memories—it was my go-to spot at the beginning of every month during my uni life in Bandung. I’d stock up on high-quality ingredients and then cook together with my dorm friends. Those were the days!

user_reviews.Description: This is a good plaza near the city square, the road is closed and for pedestrians only. What I love is there are some tables and chairs spread in the middle of the streets for people to rest and enjoy their time in plaza. But this plaza is not that crowded even tho the place is great. Imo, the retails is not really that attractive. I suggest to put some more street foods seller and manage them nicely in there. They can be spread all around the plaza with lots variant of foods so people can happyly enjoy their time there while trying Bandung's best street foods. Overall, imo this plaza have lot of potential to grow to become one of great tourism destination in Bandung.

title: Rasa Bakery & Cafe
address: Jl. Tamblong No.15, Braga, Kec. Sumur Bandung, Kota Bandung, Jawa Barat 40111

user_reviews.Name: Zain A
user_reviews.Description:
This is a retro feel bakery that feels rather homely and nice. The staffs are friendly and helpful.
The bakery itself has an old school ambience that one feels when stepping into late 90s Bandung.
It is located along the main road and there’s ample parking space inside.
The bakery has a lot of tasty offerings but i prefer the ice creams sold here.
Must try is the tutti frutti flavour that is not too sweet and has bits and pieces of fruits such as pineapple inside.
If you are in Bandung and has some time to spare, take a break here. Recommend.

user_reviews.Name: Martinus Iskandar
user_reviews.Description:
RASA cake & bakery is located on Jl. Tamblong in Bandung City. Established since 1931, this cake and bread shop is quite legendary in Bandung.
The old building's architecture has been well-preserved, giving it a vintage charm. The interior, particularly the display for cakes and bread, retains a retro feel due to its aged design. There are plenty of seats available across multiple rooms.
The menu offers a wide variety of snacks, cakes, and main courses, with many delicious options.
The Lontong Cap Go Meh with coconut milk sauce (not overly oily) and the Kecombrang fried rice are worth trying. The sausage snack filled with meat is also tasty.

RASA cake & bakery, which has been around since 1931, is also legendary for its ice cream. The ice cream has a unique taste, not overly sweet like most ice creams, making it stand out.

Prices for main courses are generally similar to those in Jakarta restaurants, while cakes and snacks are slightly above average.

user_reviews.Name: Ratu Amalia
user_reviews.Description: I've been familiar with Rasa Bakery for a long time, thanks to my mom. I've been there a few times, and Rasa's pastries are truly delicious! Unfortunately, the spices in the restaurant are a bit less bold, although the food is still tasty. But, I highly recommend Rasa Bakery to anyone visiting Bandung! 😉

user_reviews.Name: M *
user_reviews.Description:
One of the oldest cafes and bakeries in Bandung, this place has been a favorite for generations. Their nasi rames, soto ayam, soto betawi, and lontong kari are all delicious, but their ice cream is the star of the show.

The Spanish coffee is also a favorite, with their signature ice. We can choose the flavor of the ice cream. Their ice cream size has gotten smaller and smaller while the price has gone up.

This place isn’t cheap, but it has a sense of camaraderie that Bandung people have cherished for years. Their ice cream is so good that it attracts Bandungers to come back time and time again.

This is still a great place to enjoy when you’re in Bandung. The place has expanded now and still serving very nice ice cream

user_reviews.Name: Michael J.
user_reviews.Description:
One of the FnB heritage in Bandung, locals, and tourists should know this place. The concept is still using the old Dutch style of the colonial era from the front to the inside.

The parking space is very spacious, near the main road precisely. The ice cream and bakery are very worth trying and the price is enough affordable. For you who don't like ice cream is less sweet!

title: Ronde Jahe Alkateri
address: Jl. Cibadak No.99, Karang Anyar, Astanaanyar, Bandung City, West Java 40241
plus_code: 3JH2+86 Karang Anyar, Bandung City, West Java

user_reviews.Name: Art Art4
user_reviews.Description: Best Wedang Ronde so far! 🍵✨ Tried this at Cibadak and the taste is incredibly fresh—way better than what I usually find in Jakarta. A must-try if you're in the area. Will definitely be back!
user_reviews.text_original: Best Wedang Ronde so far! 🍵✨ Tried this at Cibadak and the taste is incredibly fresh—way better than what I usually find in Jakarta. A must-try if you're in the area. Will definitely be back!

user_reviews.Name: Kulineryan
user_reviews.Description:
Ronde Jahe Alkateri in Cibadak never fails to warm the body and soul with its delicious, gingery sweetness.

One portion is actually enough for two people. The mixed bowl (Rp23k) includes both small and large ronde, while the large-only version (Rp25k) features ronde with soft, creamy peanut filling. The texture is chewy, the ginger broth perfectly warming, ideal for a chilly Bandung night. You can even choose between palm sugar or white sugar for the broth; both taste amazing in their own way.

The only downside is the usual crowd of buskers and beggars around the area , typical for Cibadak, but it doesn’t take away from how good the ronde is. A true Bandung classic that never disappoints.

user_reviews.Name: Cassandra Cho
user_reviews.Description:
You don’t consider yourself completed the food hunting journey if you didn’t come here!

Unlike other ronde around Indonesia, the texture of these tang yuan (glutinuous rice ball) are soooo soft and smooth on the outside and slight chewy when you bite it. What is more surprising is the tang yuan is not sticking your teeth when chewing it. Thumbs up. This is the highest level in ronde making.

Price is affordable. The Q is long, but worth the wait if the food is good isn’t it so? Yang paling cham are those waited long Q and end up having not worth kind of food standard.

user_reviews.Name: Herin
user_reviews.Description:
Traditional dessert soup.

Food.
It’s a traditional food, ball shaped with peanuts fillings. The ball’s skin made of glutinous, and it was perfect. Not too thick, not too thin.
You can choose the big ball with peanuts, or small one without any fillings. The soup have options with brown sugar, or white sugar.

Service.
Standard, but quite good in managing the waiting time and sitting area.

Place.
Fast customer turnover, crowded. Can’t expect cleanliness as it is a street food.

Price.
A bit high, but i would say worth the food quality.

user_reviews.Name: Jeffrey B
user_reviews.Description:
[2025] Ronde Campur comes with 3 large ronde with peanut filling and 10-15 small ronde. For milder and lesser sweet, try its white sugar. More seatings now, lesser waiting time about 2 minutes, 2-3 order ahead on weekend.

[2024] As this is a famous food stall in Cibadak night culinary, you have to expect longer time & queue for both dine-in or takeaway, up to 15-20 min on busy long weekend with Jakarta tourusts flocking to try this out. The ronde is very good - soft enough and not too soft. Go for large ones with peanut fillings. The sweet ginger soup/gravy is also perfect. I prefer dark sugar mix than white sugar, as it gives more fragrance to the soup. Even though a bit pricey but worth the price.

user_reviews.Name: Eugenia Diva
user_reviews.Description:
The best ronde in Indonesia that you should definitely try when you go to @Bandung!

Their ronde is just so perfect, nice and chewy. The service is great and fast. They served my order pretty quick, but the place has limited seat, so you might need to grab a spot first.

Many people say that the price is quite expensive, however with the ronde quality, i think that it’s worth the money.

I like the “gula putih” better than the “gula merah”.

user_reviews.Name: Devina
user_reviews.Description: the best of the best of the best ronde in the world! you can ask whether you like it with brown sugar or with white sugar. You can also ask what ronde you want, the big one is the one with the nuts, and the small one is the empty one. Some people who don’t like nuts can ask the ronde with just the small one. But I like nuts, and I prefer to buy a ronde with just a big one. There’s lots of nuts in the big one, and it’s so delicious. You can add up the ginger yourself, so you can determine how ‘spicy’ you want it to be.

title: Roots Resto & Lounge
address: Jl. Gudang Selatan No.22, Merdeka, Sumurbandung, Bandung City, West Java 40113
description: Chic, energetic restaurant offering global fare, a lively bar & entertainment, plus outdoor seating.

user_reviews.Name: indra raharjo
user_reviews.Description:
**Review for Root Resto & Lounge**

Root Resto & Lounge is an excellent spot for hanging out with friends! The atmosphere is vibrant and inviting, making it perfect for a night out. One of the highlights is the live music that starts at 10 PM every night, featuring a great selection of songs that are both nostalgic and current.

The prices for food and drinks are very affordable, especially for a place in Bandung, which makes it even more appealing. You can enjoy a delightful evening without breaking the bank!

If you're looking for a fun nightlife experience, I highly recommend checking out Root Resto & Lounge. It's a must-visit for adults seeking entertainment and a lively atmosphere!

user_reviews.Name: Arnanti Primiana Yuniati
user_reviews.Description: This is the right place if you are looking for a bar that offers a piece of “chill”. They have excellent bands and DJs, moreover you can request some songs. The live music usually start around 10pm ish. They have a reasonable price for the FnB and service and plenty of parking area. The bar was a former military storages, the atmosphere is an industrial rustic combining with lights and led screen tech for visual effect. As a millennial, i love this kind of atmosphere. Haha! Although, there was a parking service, if you brought your car and parked in front of the bar, they charged you more. It was not even a valet parking. Better to reserve a table first if you go on weekend. For daily, the bar starts to get crowded around 10PM ish, but you can still get a table there by walking in.

user_reviews.Description:
More of a pub. Ambience is rustic set up.
Table cover charges so do check on it and make reservations.

Once the music starts say after 10.00pm the place is crowded and one finds themselevs in an atmosphere of smoke and vape.

The cocktails Suzzanna,pink Berries and Cocorita were good.

user_reviews.Name: Rinny Hafid
user_reviews.Description:
Came here to hang out with friends and enjoyed the Top 40 music playing throughout the night—easy, familiar vibes. Food was decent; we ordered pizza, chicken wings, and a seafood platter, all okay and suitable for sharing.

For seating, one sofa table required a minimum spend of one bottle (tequila or other premium options). We went with Bacardi and tequila, which worked well for our group. The place wasn’t overly crowded, likely because we visited on a Monday night, so it felt comfortable and relaxed.

They close at 2 AM, making it a decent spot to start the night with drinks and light bites before heading elsewhere.

user_reviews.Name: Chuck Fuller
user_reviews.Description: Found this place on Google. Bandung, being Indonesian, has places that serve Beer and liquor. But sometimes their tucked away of the main streets. The place is very unique with it's open windows, brick walls, etc. It was probably on old warehouse. Anyway, the food was good, the beer cold(and surprisingly cheap) usually you DO pay a premium to drink Alcohol, I like to say because it's a "sin" tax. Being a majority Muslim country. However, this place has a happy hour. We had pizza, oxtail soup/rice and a basket of fries. All good. Another thing about Indonesian restaurants, the servers come over after a few minutes and take All of your order, unless you tell them to bring drinks first, then appetizers. They would prefer to get everything at once. You don't tip them, so service is not on par with U.S.
user_reviews.text_original: Found this place on Google. Bandung, being Indonesian, has places that serve Beer and liquor. But sometimes their tucked away of the main streets. The place is very unique with it's open windows, brick walls, etc. It was probably on old warehouse. Anyway, the food was good, the beer cold(and surprisingly cheap) usually you DO pay a premium to drink Alcohol, I like to say because it's a "sin" tax. Being a majority Muslim country. However, this place has a happy hour. We had pizza, oxtail soup/rice and a basket of fries. All good. Another thing about Indonesian restaurants, the servers come over after a few minutes and take All of your order, unless you tell them to bring drinks first, then appetizers. They would prefer to get everything at once. You don't tip them, so service is not on par with U.S.

user_reviews.Name: Anne Burlinson
user_reviews.Description:
Feeling nostalgic and craving for sweet bread filled with sweet or savouries filling? When you're in Bandung, don't miss out on this place!

I grew up with this bread, so...they've been around for quite a while (try the 1960s). The bread is tasty and as I knew of it. I love how they maintain the old Indonesian spelling on their wrappers!

Staff members are helpful and friendly. They're happy to go the extra mile for you!

user_reviews.Name: Icha Sinaga
user_reviews.Description:
This bakery is the oldest bread shop in Bandung. The location is on Jalan Otista where parking is quite difficult. I recommend you not to bring car if you want to visit this bakery. Unless you park in other stores such as Yogya Kepatihan. You can park there and walk about 10 minutes to the bakery.
Regarding the taste, I found it is just so so. You can find other bakeries selling breads and cakes yummier than the Sidodadi's. However, if you want to experience vintage thingy, you should visit that Bandung's oldest bakery.

Most favourite product is "horn bread" With vanilla fla inside.  But i also love kaya bread, corn bread and smoked beef. The price is starting from idr 4k (for a small single)  and starting from idr 20k for whole bread. The taste was really classic.  This is a homy Products.  When you eat that bread,  you'll feel like your grandma was made it that bread.

Selling a variety of stylish, trendy women's clothing.
Fitting rooms are available, and prices are consistent with the labels.
The air conditioning isn't working properly, so it's a bit hot inside.

The sales associates provide excellent service and are very responsive to customer requests.
The only downside is that if you bring a car, you can't park in front of the store due to heavy traffic; parking is required inside the ITC building.

user_reviews.Name: nomad90125
user_reviews.Description:
If your into baking then Sukasari is the only place you need visit.
They stock ingredients from mid to top range so if your a home bake or a top chef, this shop has what you need.
user_reviews.text_original:

user_reviews.Name: sandi supriatna
user_reviews.Description: Amazing 🤩
user_reviews.text_original: Amazing 🤩

user_reviews.Name: E. A Firdaus
user_reviews.Description: Pertama kali kesini. Googling cari tempat perlengkapan kue yang lengkap di Bandung. Keluarlah salah satunya Sukasari ini. Ternyata memang belum setahun buka katanya. Beli cup case curling size M, disini 47K perroll. Satu Roll isinya 100. Kebetulan butuh banget. Kalo harga sih agak tinggi di banding Kijang Mas (40K). Tapi lebih murah di banding Ny. Liem (50K). Sempet lihat-lihat harga bahan kue lainnya. Beberapa memang lebih mahal dari yang biasa saya beli karena kami kan produksi, jadi selalu cari yang miring-miring harga. Buat yang untuk konsumsi sendiri,  bisa belanja disini. Lumayan lengkap kok. Tepung gandum juga ada. Aneka butter dan keju tersedia. Pilihan coklatnya banyak sekali. Cukup lega dan rapih tokonya. Tidak penuh pembeli jadi lebih nyaman. Bahan kue di bawah, perlengkapan baking di lantai 2. Lokasinya pas seberangnya RS. Kebon Jati.

user_reviews.text_translated: First time here. Googled for a complete cake supply store in Bandung. One of them came up in Sukasari. It turns out it hasn't been open for a year, they said. I bought a cup case curling size M, here for 47K per roll. One roll contains 100. I happened to really need it. The price is a bit high compared to Kijang Mas (40K). But it's cheaper than Ny. Liem's ​​(50K). I had a chance to look at the prices of other cake ingredients. Some are more expensive than what I usually buy because we produce, so we always look for the cheapest ones. For those for personal consumption, you can shop here. It's quite complete. Wheat flour is also available. Various butters and cheeses are available. There are lots of chocolate choices. The shop is quite spacious and neat. It's not crowded with customers so it's more comfortable. Cake ingredients are downstairs, baking supplies are on the 2nd floor. The location is right across from Kebon Jati Hospital.

user_reviews.Name: Miranti Vidyasari
user_reviews.Description:
There's a new coffee shop in Bandung that originally came from Malang (if I’m not mistaken).
Since the parking was full that day, I just grabbed an Americano to go.
It wasn't quite my favorite style of black coffee, it was bold and the nuttiness was very strong.
However, their food menu looks quite appetizing, so I might return to try their dishes.
They have both indoor and outdoor spaces, and the area is quite comfortable.

user_reviews.Name: TS Maharani
user_reviews.Description:
For the first time my Husband and I visited this "iconic" bakery at Braga street - Bandung.

I heard so much about this place, that's why we were so curious about it.

But I'm sorry, a bit disappointed. The choices was not so much... mostly it was Indonesian breads and jajanan pasar. The price was good tho not expansive. But for us it was not so much choices.

You can eat inside the bakery / take away. We decided to just take away. And the taste of the food also not extremely amazing.

I wish I made more pictures about the choices.

user_reviews.Name: Marchellin Antonia
user_reviews.Description: Located on Braga Street, this cake shop is conveniently located next to Toko Kopi Djawa. The store offers a wide variety of traditional cakes and snacks, giving you a taste of local flavors. They also have chips, candy, bread, ice cream, cold drinks, and even hot bakcang, making it a great spot for a quick snack or dessert. This place is really popular in Braga, and it tends to get quite packed, especially on the weekends. If you’re in the area, it’s definitely worth stopping by to explore the selection and enjoy some tasty treats!

user_reviews.Name: Shella Yuridasari
user_reviews.Description: A legendary cake and snack shop in Braga. They sell various traditional snacks, breads, candies, drinks. Entering their shop feels like nostalgia. I had soes rhum, pannekuk, serabi oncom, combro, pandan poffertjes and citrun candies. They all taste good.

user_reviews.Name: Hania Jasmine
user_reviews.Description:
On my last day before heading back to Jakarta, I decided to visit Braga hoping to find some good snacks. The display looked tempting and there were many varieties, which raised my expectations.

Unfortunately, it was a huge disappointment. I bought five different snacks (arem daging, risoles, bolu ketan hitam, brule bomb)  and not a single one tasted good. Even the chocolate pudding—probably the easiest item to get right—was a fail. My child didn’t like it at all, and after tasting it myself, I understood why. The flavor was off and it honestly felt like the ingredients used were very low quality.

A wide variety means nothing if the taste doesn’t deliver. Serious improvement in flavor is definitely needed

title: Waroeng Steak & Shake Lengkong Kecil
address: Jl. Lengkong Kecil No.25, Paledang, Lengkong, Bandung City, West Java 40261
description: Tempat makan steak dan shake yg halal, berdekor sederhana dan dengan harga yg tejangkau.

user_reviews.Name: Shifu Koala
user_reviews.Description:
A good place to sit down and eat heavy food in the middle of walking through the street vendors. They have a variety of meat to choose from, from chicken, sirloin, squid (I rlly wonder how this one tastes, but next time), etc. We ordered wagyu meltique, honey lemon, and caramel latte.

The wagyu meltique was tasty, though I can't say about whether it tastes like wagyu or not since I haven't had the chance to taste one. The meat is tender, juicy, and full of flavor. Personally I like sprinkling a dash of white pepper for added kick.

The drinks are good as well. Honey lemon was refreshing, a combination of sweet and sour. As for the caramel latte, I can taste the coffee in it and it's not overly sweet.

It's a cozy place, but there were a lot of buskers, beggars, and independent sellers approaching us during the one and a half hour we were there. This is no fault of the establishment, of course, as this happens to every establishment in this street.

title: Warung Nasi Ibu Imas
address: Jl. Balonggede No.69 / 48 / 93, Balonggede, Kec. Regol, Kota Bandung, Jawa Barat 40251
description: Traditional Sundanese dishes are served alongside a spicy sambal paste at this no-frills venue.

user_reviews.Name: Marcello Suryadi
user_reviews.Description:
One of the most well known culinary spot in a Bandung, and I think while the food is okay there are some issues.

First, there is not a lot of parking space available. And when you do find a space to park, when approaching this resto from afar at lunch you will see a crowd of people forming a line to eat at this place.

The line moves at a decent pace, and you are given the menu to write down your order while queuing in line. When it is your turn, you follow one of the waiter to your table. If you are seated upstairs, sometimes you can queue again on the stairs but the area is more wide and open and while there is no AC, its not as hot as you might think.

However after submitting the form, it took 30 or more minutes for the food to arrive. The waiters look tired and some (understandably) is cranky.

We ordered The Ayam Bakar, Ayam Goreng, Kikil, and their famous Jukur Goreng. And honestly, the food is pretty good. But erverything is incredibly oily. Portions are okay and price is fair.

So is this place worthed?

While the food is good I think you can find similar tasting dishes on less crowded places somewhere in Bandung. So I probably won’t be back.

user_reviews.Name: Mega Ayuningrum
user_reviews.Description: Ordered many food including fried chicken, fried eel, fried cow brain, fried cow lungs, and fried chicken intestines, fried watercress, 3 portion of rice as well as orange and tomato juice and just spend 180K IDR. Very cheap and for the price the food dims quite good. But if you there in lunch time just don't expect that you will get table soon. I came to 3 branches of this food stall (yes they do have 3 branches) and all of them are very crowded. And they have no crowd management so guests are just trying to get table themselves. Overall food is good, cheap, but the atmosphere here is so hot and crowded make it uncomfortable for group.

user_reviews.Name: Fahreza Haqie
user_reviews.Description: One of Bandung’s most sought-after culinary spot. The place looks much better after the renovation, with a great variety of sambals and vegetables to choose from. Be prepared for a bit waiting, especially at lunchtime and dinner.
user_reviews.text_original: One of Bandung’s most sought-after culinary spot. The place looks much better after the renovation, with a great variety of sambals and vegetables to choose from. Be prepared for a bit waiting, especially at lunchtime and dinner.

user_reviews.Name: Kulineryan
user_reviews.Description:
This branch of Warung Nasi Ibu Imas has finally reopened after a long renovation period. And thankfully, the renovation was worth the wait.

The place is now bigger, more spacious, and noticeably more comfortable. It feels more proper as a dining space, not just a packed warung where you eat quickly and leave. The layout is improved, the seating is better, and overall it feels upgraded without losing its original identity.

The food? Still the same. Which is exactly what you want. Solid, amazing, tasty Sundanese flavors. The portion remains generous, and the service is fast and efficient. They move quickly, which makes the dining experience smoother even when it’s busy.

And yes, even with the bigger space, it still feels crowded. But honestly, that’s not their fault. If anything, it just shows how popular this place continues to be. Demand keeps growing.

A true top-tier Sundanese restaurant in Bandung. Renovated space, consistent taste, fast service, and still packed. That’s how you know it’s legit.

user_reviews.Name: verdi anjrzq
user_reviews.Description:
Warung Bu Imas is one of the best places in Bandung to enjoy truly authentic Sundanese food. The atmosphere is simple and homey, giving you that classic local dining experience. The standout for me was the chicken — incredibly tender, juicy, and full of Sundanese flavors. It’s the kind of dish that makes you want to come back again.

Their sambal is also very memorable, freshly made with a strong kick. However, the chili level is extremely spicy, even for someone who usually enjoys heat. If you’re not used to intense spiciness, I suggest adding soy sauce to balance the flavor. Once you mix it in, the sambal becomes more enjoyable without losing its authentic taste.

Portions are generous, the food quality is consistent, and the prices are very reasonable, making it great value for money. You get delicious, satisfying dishes without having to spend much, which is why so many people love coming here. Despite the crowd, service is fast and efficient, and the staff are friendly.

Overall, Warung Bu Imas is an excellent spot for anyone craving bold and delicious Sundanese food at a good price. Just be ready for the sambal — it definitely packs a serious punch!

user_reviews.Name: Yanly Riky
user_reviews.Description:
Warung Bu Imas is a really popular spot in Bandung, so we decided to check it out. We arrived around 11 AM, which was a good call because it wasn't packed yet. The place has a lively, casual vibe, and it’s pretty cool seeing all the different dishes laid out. We grabbed a few things that looked good to try out.

We ordered the Ayam Bakar, Sayur Asem, and Tahu Goreng. Honestly, for how famous and busy this place gets, I felt the food was just okay. The Ayam Bakar tasted like a pretty standard grilled chicken you could find anywhere else—nothing that made it stand out. The sambal was spicy, but it didn't have any unique flavor that made it special.

The Sayur Asem was also a bit of a letdown for me. It wasn't very sour anymore and actually tasted a little sweet, which was a bit strange for a dish that's supposed to be tangy. While the food wasn't bad, I don't know if I'd say it's worth the potential long wait and the slightly higher-than-average prices. It was a decent meal, but not exactly what I was hoping for.

user_reviews.Name: Annisa
user_reviews.Description:
I’ve heard so many good things about Bu Imas, so I finally gave it a try. To be honest, I feel like it’s a bit overrated.

Here’s why:
1) There’s a queue even though they’ve renovated and expanded. I still ended up eating in the older building, which wasn’t very clean when I sat down.
2) The fried chicken tasted just like other places — nothing particularly special.
3)Their sambal is very spicy — if you love heat, you might enjoy it.
4) The real highlight for me was the jukut goreng — super tasty and unique!⭐️⭐️⭐️

Overall, it’s affordable and I’d probably order again, but next time I’ll go through Grab/Gojek instead of dining in.

user_reviews.Name: julia
user_reviews.Description:
This place is MUST whenever I visit Bandung!
I ordered nasi with fried chicken and a side of tempe bacem, tahu goreng, perkedel jagung and jukut goreng. When eaten altogether it tastes amazing. The different flavors and textures like the crispy jukut goreng and the juicy ayam goreng paired with the sambal will have you finishing your plate clean!
user_reviews.text_original:
This place is MUST whenever I visit Bandung!
I ordered nasi with fried chicken and a side of tempe bacem, tahu goreng, perkedel jagung and jukut goreng. When eaten altogether it tastes amazing. The different flavors and textures like the crispy jukut goreng and the juicy ayam goreng paired with the sambal will have you finishing your plate clean!

user_reviews.Name: Kulineryan
user_reviews.Description:
This place is basically a Sundanese institution. If you grew up in Bandung, there’s a high chance you’ve sat at one of their tables at some point in your life. On this one street alone, there are at least four branches, and all of them are packed every single day. That says something.

I’ve been coming here since I was in elementary school because my school was nearby. My grandma and my mom used to bring me, so the nostalgia hits hard. People like to say the taste isn’t the same anymore, that it’s not as special as it used to be. For me, it still hits exactly the same. Still tasty, still comforting, still satisfying in the most Sundanese way possible.

Their big three are non-negotiable: sambal dadak, karedok leunca, and ayam bakar. If you don’t order those, then you’re doing Ibu Imas wrong. But there’s more to explore. Their usus goreng is crispy and addictive, otak sapi goreng is flavorful, pepes always fragrant, perkedel jagung warm and homey, paru goreng tasty, and jukut goreng surprisingly solid.

Warung Nasi Ibu Imas isn’t just a meal. It’s a Bandung staple, a memory keeper, and a guaranteed good time for anyone craving Sundanese comfort food done right.
